generated_parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
generated_parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements: discipline: integration - role: Wing/fuselage datums, harness raceways, hatch access, connection types and load-path closure
[replaceable_module_path] good: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown.
[cable_harness_visibility] good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels.
[access_not_just_text] good: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover.

[harness_strain_relief_and_bend_radius] good: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges.
[connector_accessibility_and_keying] good: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.
[datum_visibility] good: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled.
[clearance_named] good: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances.
[interface_count] good: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums.
[standard_fit_vocabulary] good: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance.
[standard_hardware_dimensioning] good: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters).
[wall_process_readiness] good: Material/process, wall assumptions, build direction, and supports are explicit enough for design review.
[inspection_traceability] good: Verify/risk items reference real features or interfaces from part_tree/geometry_ops.
[risk_coverage] good: Risks cover blocked access, weak interfaces, tolerance mismatch, process limitations, and unsafe overclaiming.
[requirement_to_verification_matrix] good: Each stated requirement maps to a verification method (inspection, demonstration, analysis, test) tied to a named feature or interface.
[interface_control_evidence] good: Cross-subsystem interfaces state owner, mating geometry, and what is exchanged (load, power, data, fluid), like a miniature interface control entry.

[connection_type_named] good: Every inter-part interface names a specific connection type (bolted, threaded_insert, snap_fit, press_fit, dovetail_slide, clevis_pin, revolute_bearing, band_clamp, adhesive_lap, o_ring, connector_mate, harness_passthrough) with its DOF and a numeric clearance.

[assembly_sequence_feasible] good: Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable; no installed part blocks access to a later one; last-installed and captive parts are identified.

[datum_chain_continuity] good: One primary datum part anchors the assembly; each other part locates off a named parent datum using a 3-2-1 scheme, so tolerance does not accumulate uncontrolled across the stack.
[load_path_closure] good: Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground; the load path closes for all parts and high-load joints use load-appropriate connection types.

[structural_development_direction] good: Compared to the previous version the design moves the right direction: fewer interfaces, parts consolidated where service is preserved, open load paths closed, and redundancy added only at critical joints.

[interface_minimization] good: The assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture; each remaining interface is justified by service, motion, manufacture split, or load transfer.

verify.rule: All required evidence_features are present.
Mass estimate matches suggested design.
Each P0 feature is mapped to a geometry_op.
Interfaces match neighboring subsystems.
Critical joints have visible locking/preload evidence.

risk.desc: Potential for harness strain damage due to unsupported cable exits.
risk.mit: Ensure all cable exits have strain relief geometry.

risk.desc: Risk of incorrect connector mating due to lack of keying.
risk.mit: Implement keying or labeling on all connectors.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance for moving parts may cause jamming.
risk.mit: Specify clearances for all critical interfaces.

risk.desc: Potential for assembly sequence issues if parts are not accessible.
risk.mit: Define a feasible assembly sequence with part reachability.

risk.desc: Risk of uncontrolled tolerance accumulation in datum chain.
risk.mit: Establish a primary datum part and ensure continuity.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing/fuselage datums, harness raceways, hatch access, connection types and load-path closure
